items.summary: QueryExecutor declares executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern as default methods that fall back to lower-level calls, but there is only one real implementation, making the defaults unused scaffolding

evidence: QueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery() default: return executeQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults) — comment says 'for backward compatibility'
QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern() default: return findCollocations(lemma, bcqlPattern, ...) — comment says 'ignores position hints'
BlackLabQueryExecutor overrides both, so the defaults are never exercised in the production code path
getExecutorType() default returns getClass().getSimpleName() — useful pattern, but the other two defaults only exist to ease a non-existent alternate implementation
detail.suggestion: If only one implementation exists, declare executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern as abstract interface methods (no defaults) to make the contract explicit. Remove the backward-compatibility comment which implies an obsolete migration that is already complete.

plan_cluster.name: design-structural
plan_cluster.description: Larger structural improvements: package reorganization, SEF constructor injection, QueryExecutor interface cleanup, SnippetParser API consolidation, SimpleTagger lexicon externalization

action_steps.title: Clean up migration debt in GrammarConfigLoader and QueryExecutor
action_steps.detail: 268346c4: Fix GrammarConfigLoader Javadoc to show 'pattern' as canonical field. 4bf87c03: Add logger.warn deprecation notice for tag= legacy branches. aa87d189: Add @Deprecated to QueryExecutor backward-compat default methods. e2ee35a0: Make executeBcqlQuery/executeSurfacePattern abstract (remove defaults) since only one impl exists. 24b898b3: Move amp entity replacement into HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams or remove if not needed.

action_steps.title: Fix initialization coupling: null grammarConfig propagation and hardcoded paths
action_steps.detail: a32123fd: Fail fast at startup if grammar config is missing — throw in Main.java rather than propagating null. e469c854: Accept --grammar CLI option in handleServerCommand, use hardcoded path as default only. 595bb84c: Move HttpServer creation into constructor or add state guard in start(). c19c157d: Consider factory methods for BlackLabQueryExecutor/GrammarConfigLoader to separate construction from I/O.

action_steps.title: Refactor executeBcqlQuery phases and fix BlackLabQueryExecutor SnippetParser duplication
action_steps.detail: 8ed8f0b8: Extract private collectHits(...) and buildResults(hits, freqMap, headFreq, maxResults) helpers from executeBcqlQuery's 3 inlined phases. ab68f7f2: Remove extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup; replace with BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch and extractPosFromMatch calls.

action_steps.title: Package organization and SimpleTagger lexicon externalization
action_steps.detail: 82270299/a430f585: Move BlackLabSnippetParser to query/blacklab/, move QueryResults to model/. 297b2b52: Remove string-path constructor from SEF or convert to factory method. 42e2a9a1: Move SEF inner result classes to separate files or model/. 28bc8918: Extract SEF result types to their own file. 321f810b: Consolidate SnippetParser fragmented extraction API. 69e0017b: Move 93 lexicon.put() calls to classpath resource file.

items.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser exposes seven near-identical methods for extracting lemmas from XML or text at a given position, creating a fragmented API with overlapping responsibilities

evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch(String matchText) — finds last lemma in XML
extractCollocateFromSnippet(String snippet) — also finds last lemma in XML
extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(String xmlSnippet, int position) — finds lemma at 1-based position in XML
extractCollocateFromMatch(String matchOnly, int labelPos) — same as above, different name
extractTokenFromSnippet(String snippet, int position) — same pattern, falls back to last lemma
extractCollocateFromMatchText(String matchText, int position) — whitespace-separated variant
extractCollocateFromPlainText(String text, int position) — another whitespace-separated variant
extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateFromSnippet do exactly the same thing (iterate LEMMA_ATTR, return last)
detail.suggestion: Consolidate to two clear methods: extractLemmaAtPosition(String xml, int position) for XML and extractTokenAtPosition(String text, int position) for plain text. Remove duplicates and document the contract (0 = last, n = nth).

items.summary: WordSketchApiServer imports five inner result classes from SemanticFieldExplorer, tightly coupling the API layer to exploration implementation internals

evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective
All five are accessed by field name (e.g., dn.noun, dn.sharedCount, ca.adjective) in the API handlers, encoding the data model in two layers simultaneously
Any rename or restructuring of SemanticFieldExplorer's result classes requires changes in the API layer
detail.suggestion: Expose SemanticFieldExplorer results through stable accessor methods or move the result types to a shared results package so the API layer does not depend on inner-class details of SemanticFieldExplorer.

items.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer's primary constructor directly instantiates BlackLabQueryExecutor, bypassing the QueryExecutor abstraction it also accepts

evidence: Line 71: public SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) throws IOException { this.executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); }
Line 76: A second constructor takes QueryExecutor (for testing), showing the abstraction exists but the production path bypasses it
The ownsExecutor flag is a code smell indicating lifecycle management is being manually layered on top of an inconsistent creation pattern
detail.suggestion: Remove the String-path constructor or make it a static factory; let callers always inject a QueryExecutor. If lifecycle ownership is needed, provide an explicit factory method SemanticFieldExplorer.forIndex(String) that documents the ownership contract.

items.summary: executeBcqlQuery in BlackLabQueryExecutor conflates three distinct phases in one 100-line method: hit collection, collocate frequency accumulation, and result ranking

evidence: Lines 228-277: single pass collecting HitRecord objects and building collocateFreqMap
Lines 280-308: second pass computing logDice and building ConcordanceResult list
The method also handles collocate extraction fallback logic inline, competing with BlackLabSnippetParser's responsibilities
detail.suggestion: Extract collectHits(...) and buildResults(List<HitRecord>, Map<String,Long>, long, int) private helpers, giving each phase a named boundary.

items.summary: The query package subsumes executor infrastructure, result DTOs, snippet parsing utilities, semantic field exploration logic, and POS group constants — too many concerns for a single package

evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor: query execution infrastructure
BlackLabSnippetParser: XML/text parsing utility
SemanticFieldExplorer: multi-step exploration domain logic (700+ lines with its own inner result class hierarchy)
QueryResults: shared result DTOs
PosGroup: domain constants
detail.suggestion: Split the query package: keep query execution infrastructure in query/, move SemanticFieldExplorer + its result types to exploration/ or semantic/, move BlackLabSnippetParser to query/internal or parsing/, and move PosGroup to a shared constants package.

items.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer serves as both a service (exploration/comparison logic) and a result data container (6 static inner result classes), blurring its responsibility

evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer contains: ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge — all static inner classes
The service methods exploreByPattern() and compare() are in the same file as 6 result/DTO types totalling ~300 lines of inner class definitions
API layer must import individual inner classes: import SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, etc.
detail.suggestion: Extract the result types (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult, etc.) to their own top-level classes or a dedicated results sub-package. SemanticFieldExplorer should only contain the service logic.

items.summary: GrammarConfigLoader silently falls back to the old 'cql_pattern' JSON field when 'pattern' is absent, but the class Javadoc still documents the old field name as canonical

evidence: String pattern = relObj.containsKey("pattern") ? relObj.getString("pattern") : (relObj.containsKey("cql_pattern") ? relObj.getString("cql_pattern") : "");
The class-level Javadoc example shows '"cql_pattern": "[tag=jj.*]"' — the old field name — as the expected structure
No deprecation warning is emitted when the fallback path is taken, making it invisible at runtime
detail.suggestion: Either remove the cql_pattern fallback if no live configs use it, or emit a deprecation warning via logger.warn when it is used, and update the Javadoc to show 'pattern' as the canonical field

items.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.posGroupFromConstraint handles both 'xpos=' (current) and 'tag=' (legacy) attribute names with no migration path or deprecation signal

evidence: Comment: '// xpos (Penn Treebank — used by current grammar)' followed by 'tag (legacy / alternative attribute name)'
Twelve tag= branches mirror twelve xpos= branches without any log warning when the legacy path triggers
No test or comment indicates whether any active grammar config file still uses tag= attributes
detail.suggestion: Audit active grammar JSON files; if tag= is unused, remove the legacy branches and their comment; if still in use, add a logger.warn deprecation notice

items.summary: QueryExecutor default methods exist solely for backward compatibility with implementations that no longer exist

evidence: executeBcqlQuery default: '// Default: treat BCQL as CQL (for backward compatibility)'
executeSurfacePattern default: '// Default: fall back to findCollocations (ignores position hints)'
QueryExecutor Javadoc: 'Allows different implementations (legacy, hybrid, BlackLab) to be used interchangeably' — but only BlackLabQueryExecutor exists in the codebase
getExecutorType return example says '"legacy", "hybrid"' — neither type is implemented
detail.suggestion: Remove the default implementations and the mention of legacy/hybrid; if the interface is meant to allow future alternative implementations, document that intent rather than preserving dead compatibility code

items.summary: Both BlackLabQueryExecutor and GrammarConfigLoader perform file I/O in their constructors, mixing construction with resource acquisition

evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor: this.blackLabIndex = BlackLab.open(new File(indexPath)); — opens an index file during construction
GrammarConfigLoader constructor: String content = Files.readString(configPath); — reads and parses the config file during construction

GrammarConfigLoader constructor also throws IllegalArgumentException for business rule violations (e.g. missing 'version', presence of 'copulas') mixed with IOException for file access failures; callers must handle both exception types from a single constructor call
detail.suggestion: For BlackLabQueryExecutor, provide a static factory method or an open() method distinct from the constructor; for GrammarConfigLoader, separate file loading from validation so that IOException and IllegalArgumentException come from clearly distinct call sites

items.summary: The grammar config path 'grammars/relations.json' is resolved relative to the JVM working directory at startup, creating a fragile environment dependency

evidence: var grammarPath = java.nio.file.Paths.get("grammars/relations.json"); — no ability to override from CLI
GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() also hardcodes Path.of("grammars/relations.json")
The server command does accept --index for the corpus path but provides no equivalent --grammar option, forcing the grammar file to live at a specific CWD-relative location
detail.suggestion: Add a --grammar CLI option to handleServerCommand so operators can specify the config path; use the hardcoded path only as a default, and log the resolved absolute path at startup to aid debugging

action_steps.detail: 82270299/a430f585: Move BlackLabSnippetParser to query/blacklab/, move QueryResults to model/. 297b2b52: Remove string-path constructor from SEF or convert to factory method. 42e2a9a1: Move SEF inner result classes to separate files or model/. 28bc8918: Extract SEF result types to their own file. 321f810b: Consolidate SnippetParser fragmented extraction API. 69e0017b: Move 93 lexicon.put() calls to classpath resource file.

items.summary: A null GrammarConfigLoader is passed through from Main into WordSketchApiServer and then null-checked in every single endpoint handler

evidence: Main.handleServerCommand: GrammarConfigLoader grammarConfig = null; try { ... } catch (IOException e) { System.out.println("No grammar config found, using defaults."); } — null is silently accepted
WordSketchApiServer receives grammarConfig as a constructor parameter and stores it as a nullable field

Every handler that uses grammar config: 'if (grammarConfig != null)' — at least 6 distinct null guards in handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
Without grammar config, most endpoints either silently return empty results or return a 500 error — the behavior is inconsistent
detail.suggestion: Fail fast at startup if grammar config is missing (the server cannot serve meaningful results without it); alternatively use a non-null NullObject/default implementation rather than propagating null through the entire API layer

items.summary: WordSketchApiServer stores the com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer instance in a field set during start(), leaving the object partially initialized between construction and start()

evidence: private com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer server; — field is null until start() is called
stop() method: if (server != null) { server.stop(0); } — null guard confirms caller-visible partial-init state
start() has no guard against being called twice; a second call would create a new server object while the first continues running and the original reference is lost
detail.suggestion: Either create the HttpServer inside the constructor (before start()) so the object is fully initialized when returned, or use a builder pattern; add an idempotency guard in start() that throws IllegalStateException if called when already running

items.summary: RadialPlot.toSVG() mixes string concatenation with StringBuilder.append and String.format in an inconsistent style within the same method

evidence: Line 163: svg.append("<svg ... width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ..." — string concatenation inside append
Line 177: svg.append("  <rect width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ...) — same pattern
Line 168: svg.append(String.format("    .guide-circle { ...stroke-width: %s; }\n", fmt(...))) — uses String.format
Line 163 and 177 could use String.format or a template for consistency with adjacent lines
detail.suggestion: Pick one string-building style for the SVG template lines. Using String.format consistently (already used for most lines) is clearest. Alternatively, use a simple template with placeholders and replace all at once.

items.summary: SimpleTagger's constructor contains ~80 hard-coded lexicon.put() calls for word-to-tag mappings, embedding test corpus data as production code

evidence: SimpleTagger() constructor lines 113-210 contain explicit put() calls for words like house, dog, cat, park, bed, hill, man, garden, etc.
Comments like '// More adjectives from test corpus' and '// Common nouns' confirm this is test data baked into production code
The loadLexicon(String file) method already exists to load from a file, but the constructor pre-populates with hard-coded entries instead of delegating to it
detail.suggestion: Move the hard-coded entries to a classpath resource file (e.g., /tagger/default_lexicon.txt). Load it in the constructor with loadLexicon(). This keeps the code clean and makes the lexicon maintainable without recompilation.

items.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup duplicate BlackLabSnippetParser logic by directly accessing its static Pattern fields

evidence: extractDependentLemmaFromGroup opens a Matcher on BlackLabSnippetParser.LEMMA_ATTR and iterates to find the last lemma — identical to BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch
extractPosFromGroup opens a Matcher on BlackLabSnippetParser.XPOS_ATTR then falls back to UPOS_ATTR — identical to BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch
BlackLabSnippetParser already exposes extractLemmaFromMatch(String) and extractPosFromMatch(String) as package-private statics for exactly this purpose
detail.suggestion: Delete extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup; replace their call sites with BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch(identity) and BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch(identity)

items.summary: handleSemanticFieldExplore calls rel.getFullPattern(seed) to substitute the headword, but handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti uses the raw rel.pattern() without substitution

evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed);
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern(); (no head substitution)
Both handlers pass bcqlPattern to executor.executeSurfacePattern, meaning explore-multi queries execute with an unsubstituted template pattern
detail.suggestion: In handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, derive the per-seed bcql pattern inside the seed loop via rel.getFullPattern(seed) rather than using the raw pattern once before the loop

items.summary: handleConcordanceExamples manually replaces HTML entity & after URL decoding, a concern that belongs in the parsing layer

evidence: if (word1 != null) word1 = word1.replace("&", "&");
if (word2 != null) word2 = word2.replace("&", "&");
HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams already handles URLDecoder.decode; HTML entity decoding is a separate concern that should either be handled there or not at all
detail.suggestion: Move HTML entity unescaping into HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams or a shared normalization helper so handlers receive clean values without additional glue code

items.summary: The query/ package conflates four distinct concerns: query execution, XML parsing, data-transfer objects, and semantic exploration

evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor: query execution against a BlackLab index
BlackLabSnippetParser: stateless XML/text transformation utility for parsing BlackLab output
QueryResults: plain DTO classes (WordSketchResult, ConcordanceResult) with no query logic
SemanticFieldExplorer: a higher-level exploration algorithm that composes query results
PosGroup: a constants file imported by config/ and api/ packages, not a query-layer concern
detail.suggestion: Extract BlackLabSnippetParser into a query/blacklab/ or utils/blacklab/ sub-package; move QueryResults DTOs to a model/ package; move PosGroup to a shared model/ or common/ package; consider a query/explore/ sub-package for SemanticFieldExplorer

clusters.name: api-coherence
clusters.description: Fix API coherence: BCQL error returns HTTP 200 (16250503); extract duplicate relation switch (90d6143d).

clusters.name: abstraction-cleanup
clusters.description: Remove hollow abstractions: dead Token match methods (7de930fa) and hollow PosTagger interface (5f270cc8).

clusters.name: convention-fixes
clusters.description: Fix convention issues: replace System.out with logger in BlackLabConllUIndexer, convert RelationsValidationTest to JUnit, fix test naming convention (Test prefix), remove debug prefix from logger.debug() calls, make BlackLabSnippetParser public, standardize wildcard imports

clusters.name: migration-cleanup
clusters.description: Clean up incomplete migration artifacts: .bak files, phantom class references in CLAUDE.md, stale javadoc in QueryResults, orphaned SketchGrammarParser and CQL grammar classes, dead predicate subsystem

clusters.name: design-fixes
clusters.description: Fix design defects: double-close bug, stream-to-logger bug, duplicate logDice pipeline (4x in BlackLabQueryExecutor), duplicate derivePosition methods, double query execution, REPITITION typo, duplicate split methods, dual relation lookup API, inconsistent InvalidQuery handling

clusters.name: test-fixes
clusters.description: Fix test infrastructure: RelationsValidationTest uses main() not @Test; LongIntHashMapTest is in wrong package; integration tests use hardcoded paths; CQL verifier has shallow assertions; dummy test has no assertions

clusters.name: package-org
clusters.description: Structural / documentation issues: utils catch-all package, query package blob (too many classes), phantom /api/grammar/active endpoint, README startup commands incomplete, sketch query params undocumented, GrammarConfigLoader god constructor, oversized executeBcqlQuery, god class API server

clusters.name: bugs-first
clusters.description: Fix genuine correctness bugs: multi-seed pattern substitution missing, SimpleTagger 'am' tagged wrong, remaining parseInt sites

clusters.name: quick-wins
clusters.description: Trivial one-liner fixes: remove unused variable, add @Override, remove dead code, fix pom.xml artifact, remove trivial Javadoc

clusters.name: error-handling
clusters.description: Fix resource leaks and silent exception swallowing: try-with-resources for SEF, unify exception types in GrammarConfigLoader

clusters.name: stubs-cleanup
clusters.description: Resolve getCorpusSize stub: either implement using BlackLab API or remove and add to interface as not-implemented

clusters.name: god-class-extraction
clusters.description: Break WordSketchApiServer monolith: extract duplicate handlers, try-with-resources, extract response builder helper

clusters.name: design-structural
clusters.description: Larger structural improvements: package reorganization, SEF constructor injection, QueryExecutor interface cleanup, SnippetParser API consolidation, SimpleTagger lexicon externalization
